rationale: Bellezza et al. is used repeatedly in the core literature review and is explicitly summarized in Table 1, not just mentioned once in background. The citing paper positions its contribution relative to this prior upgrade work by arguing that earlier studies, including Bellezza et al., did not test how upgrades affect owners' brand preference and consumer-brand relationship distance. Thus, the citation helps establish the paper's research gap and hypothesis framing in a meaningful way.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the product-upgrade line represented by Bellezza et al. (2017) by shifting from upgrade-induced carelessness toward possessions to upgrade-induced changes in brand preference among current owners. It adds ownership status and perceived relationship distance as key moderator/mediator variables and examines brand-level outcomes.
